sections.text: Every word on this page was suggested by a visitor and applied by an AI agent through a real GitHub pull request. Suggest a change, watch the pipeline run, see the page update.

items: Moderation — Claude Haiku checks the suggestion for safety and relevance
Generation — Claude Sonnet produces a structured patch
Validation — Zod schemas enforce the patch shape before any commit
Commit — Octokit opens a pull request against the repository
Merge — CI runs the allowlist check; if it passes, GitHub auto-merges
Deploy — Vercel rebuilds; the page updates within 60 seconds

sections.title: The agent cannot break the site
sections.body: A GitHub Actions workflow blocks any pull request that touches files outside a strict allowlist. The agent can only modify structured data — never application code, never component files.

sections.title: What the agent cannot do
sections.body: The agent cannot invent new section types, write arbitrary HTML, or modify styling. It manipulates structured data that pre-built components know how to render. Constraints are the point.

sections.text: Structural edits mean the agent can split one paragraph into three, merge sections, reorder them, add a new callout, or remove a section entirely — not just swap words. The page shape itself is editable.

sections.code: agent(elevator-pitch): rewrite opening paragraph to active voice

items: "Add a section about the kill switch"
"Put the pipeline steps after the callout"
"Make the safety callout a success tone"

sections.text: The thing is interesting because it's real, not because it claims to be.
Every change has a real pull request →
